description: Harden executeInTab and dispatch result validation across all browser tools. PR #38 fixed 2 of 10 vulnerable call sites in analyze-site and 1 in extension-get-logs. This PRD completes the hardening by adding a centralized truncated-JSON guard in executeInTab and applying proper type validation to all remaining unsafe call sites.

userStories.title: Add truncated-JSON guard inside executeInTab
userStories.description: Add a guard in executeInTab that detects when the return value is a truncated JSON string (starts with '{' or '[') instead of a parsed object/array, and returns null instead. This protects all callers from the Chrome truncation bug at once.
acceptanceCriteria: executeInTab returns null (not the truncated string) when inner.value is a string starting with '{' or '['
A warning is logged when truncation is detected, including the script length and truncated value length
Existing callers that use `?? default` fall back correctly when null is returned
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/analyze-site/index.ts (modify executeInTab function at line 68)

Context:
executeInTab is a local helper (not exported) that wraps dispatchToExtension for browser.executeScript. It unwraps the nested result and returns `inner?.value ?? null`. When Chrome truncates large payloads (>50KB), the value arrives as a truncated JSON string like `'{"localEntries":[{"key":"foo"...'` instead of the parsed object.

Current code at line 83:
```
return inner?.value ?? null;
```

Changes:
Before the return on line 83, add a guard:
```ts
const value = inner?.value;
if (typeof value === 'string' && value.length > 0 && (value[0] === '{' || value[0] === '[')) {
  // Chrome truncated a large JSON payload to a string — return null so callers fall back to defaults
  return null;
}
return value ?? null;
```

Do NOT add a console.warn or logger call — this is a server-side helper with no logger in scope. The null return is sufficient since all callers have fallback defaults.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the function signature or return type
- Do NOT add logging infrastructure — keep it simple
- The guard should only trigger for strings that look like truncated JSON, not for legitimate string return values (e.g., `return document.title` returns a plain string that does not start with '{' or '[')
- pageTitle call at line 954 legitimately returns a string — must not be affected

userStories.title: Harden array-type executeInTab call sites in analyze-site
userStories.description: Apply Array.isArray() validation to the 7 executeInTab call sites that expect arrays, matching the pattern established by PR #38 for storageKeys/storageEntries.
acceptanceCriteria: frameworkProbes (line 862) uses Array.isArray() before passing to deduplicateFrameworkProbes
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/analyze-site/index.ts (7 call sites between lines 846-912)

Context:
These 7 call sites all use the same vulnerable pattern: `((await executeInTab(...)) ?? []) as SomeType[]`. If executeInTab returns a non-array (e.g., truncated string), the `?? []` doesn't trigger (string is truthy) and the `as` cast silently lies. Downstream code iterates expecting objects but gets string characters.

US-001 adds a centralized guard, but defense-in-depth means each call site should also validate.

Changes:
For each call site, replace the inline cast with an Array.isArray check. Pattern:

Before:
```ts
csrfTokens = ((await executeInTab(state, tabId, CSRF_SCRIPT)) ?? []) as CsrfDomToken[];
```

After:
```ts
const rawCsrf = await executeInTab(state, tabId, CSRF_SCRIPT);
csrfTokens = Array.isArray(rawCsrf) ? (rawCsrf as CsrfDomToken[]) : [];
```

Apply this pattern to all 7 sites:
1. Line 848: `csrfTokens` — CSRF_SCRIPT → CsrfDomToken[]
2. Line 855: `globalsAuth` — GLOBALS_AUTH_SCRIPT → GlobalEntry[]
3. Line 862-864: `frameworkProbes` — FRAMEWORK_PROBE_SCRIPT → FrameworkProbe[] (wrap the Array.isArray check BEFORE passing to deduplicateFrameworkProbes)
4. Line 887: `globalsScan` — GLOBALS_SCAN_SCRIPT → GlobalProperty[]
5. Line 894: `forms` — FORMS_SCRIPT → FormInput[]
6. Line 901: `interactiveElements` — INTERACTIVE_ELEMENTS_SCRIPT → InteractiveElementInput[]
7. Line 909: `dataAttributes` — DATA_ATTRIBUTES_SCRIPT → string[]

Constraints:
- Do NOT modify the storageKeys (line 920-932) or storageEntries (line 940-950) blocks — those were already hardened by PR #38
- Do NOT modify the pageTitle call (line 954) — it legitimately returns a string
- Keep each change within the existing try/catch block
- Preserve the empty-array default for each variable

userStories.title: Harden spaSsrProbe object-type executeInTab call site
userStories.description: Apply object shape validation to the spaSsrProbe executeInTab call site (line 878) which expects an object with boolean properties, not an array.
acceptanceCriteria: spaSsrProbe call site validates result is a non-null, non-array object before assignment
Each boolean property (hasSingleRootElement, usesPushState, hasNextData, hasNuxtData, hasHydrationMarkers) is validated with typeof check or defaults to false
Falls back to defaultSpaSsrProbe if result is not the expected shape

userStories.title: Harden extension-get-logs stats and extension-get-state/check-adapter result spreads
userStories.description: Fix unsafe type casts in extension-get-logs (stats arithmetic), extension-get-state (result spread), and extension-check-adapter (result spread).
acceptanceCriteria: extension-get-logs validates data.stats is a non-null object with numeric properties before arithmetic
extension-get-state validates r.result is a non-null, non-array object before spreading
extension-check-adapter validates r.result is a non-null, non-array object before spreading
